channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Icon Size Guidelines for Web and Mobile Applications
item.description: Every developer has faced the challenge of choosing the right icon sizes for web and mobile apps. Too small, and icons become hard to tap or recognize; too large, and they crowd the interface....

item.title: I Built a CLI That Sets Up tRPC v11 in Next.js With One Command
item.description: I Built a CLI That Sets Up tRPC v11 in Next.js With One Command Every time I started a new Next.js project with tRPC, I found myself doing the same thing — copying the same 6 files, installing the...

item.title: 9 Privacy-First Mac Apps Built with SwiftUI & CoreML — No Cloud Required
item.description: I’ve been exploring a portfolio of indie Mac/iOS apps built by a solo developer — all sharing the same philosophy: your data stays on your device. No accounts, no uploads, no cloud dependency....

item.title: 7 ITSM Tools Developers Should Evaluate in 2026
item.description: 7 ITSM Tools Developers Should Evaluate in 2026 IT Service Management (ITSM) for DevOps is a framework that connects change management, infrastructure visibility, incident tracking, and compliance...

item.title: Smart Toolbar Pagination with Input
item.description: Pagination is one of those components that seems simple — until you actually build it properly. Most pagination components show every page number and break completely on mobile. I built one that's...

item.title: Java String Methods You'll Actually Use Every Day
item.description: I remember when I first started learning Java, strings felt weirdly complicated. Like, why so many methods? Which ones do I even need? Turns out, you only need a handful 90% of the time. Here they...

item.title: The Royal Gazette: A Single-Line Noble Intrigue
item.description: This is a submission for the Built with Google Gemini: Writing Challenge Sorry I maded this submission under time pressure spend just a 2 hour to make it. All things created gemini, I even don't...

item.title: What Changed When Our Production NLU Pipeline Hit a Hard Ceiling (A Live Case Study)
item.description: Two months into a high-growth quarter, the natural language stack that powered a customer-facing pipeline stopped scaling. On 2025-08-12 the system responsible for intent classification, response...

item.title: When Image Models Grew Up: Practical Moves for Teams Choosing the Right Generator
item.description: For a long while the conversation about image models read like a race card: bigger networks, more parameters, and a checklist of benchmark wins. That framing glossed over the real work engineers...

item.title: Building an Automated Growth Engine: How We Replaced Ad-Tech "Guesswork" with an AI+BI Architecture
item.description: If you’ve ever worked close to the marketing or growth teams in a tech company, you’ve probably noticed a glaring engineering bottleneck: Manual Operations. While developers obsess over CI/CD...

item.title: APT41-Linked Silver Dragon Targets Governments Using Cobalt Strike and Google Drive C2
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an advanced persistent threat (APT) group dubbed Silver Dragon that has been linked to cyber attacks targeting entities in Europe and Southeast...

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited VMware Aria Operations Flaw CVE-2026-22719 to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting Broadcom VMware Aria Operations to its Known Exploited...

item.title: Why AI startups are selling the same equity at two different prices
item.description: Some AI founders are using a novel valuation mechanism to manufacture unicorn status.

item.title: Anker’s last-gen sleep buds are nearly 40 percent off ahead of daylight saving time
item.description: Bad news: most Americans are about to lose an hour of sleep next week. Good news: if you have trouble falling (or staying asleep), Newegg is currently selling Anker’s Soundcore Sleep A20 earbuds...

item.title: Does Ticketmaster have a stranglehold on concert ticketing — or is it just ‘bringing joy’?
item.description: In a downtown Manhattan courtroom on Monday, lawyers for the US Justice Department and 40 state and district attorneys general warned a jury that the concert industry was being squeezed by a...

item.title: Alibaba’s Qwen tech lead steps down after major AI push
item.description: Reactions rippled through Alibaba's Qwen team after tech lead Junyang Lin stepped down following a major model launch.

item.title: Downdetector, Speedtest sold to IT service provider Accenture in $1.2B deal
item.description: Accenture plans to buy Ookla, which also includes RootMetrics and Ekahau.

item.title: AI companies are spending millions to thwart this former tech exec’s congressional bid
item.description: A tech billionaire-backed super PAC is spending $125 million to undercut candidates pushing for AI regulation. New York's Alex Bores, a former tech executive himself, is one of them.

item.title: What Trump’s war on Iran means for the US energy crunch
item.description: Fuel prices surged after the Trump administration launched strikes against Iran on Saturday, immediately raising questions about whether the war would increase energy costs for Americans, put more...

item.title: The best AirPods deals you can get right now
item.description: If you know where to look, you can often score deals on Apple’s ever-expanding AirPods lineup. Both the AirPods Max and AirPods 4 (with and without ANC) now consistently receive discounts, as do...

item.title: Another Oracle outage is messing up US TikTok
item.description: The US version of TikTok is once again experiencing issues due to an Oracle outage, just a month after coming back online from a similar outage in February. The service disruption, impacting...

item.title: ChatGPT’s new GPT-5.3 Instant model will stop telling you to calm down
item.description: The company says the new model will reduce the "cringe" that's been annoying its users for months.

item.title: Highguard is shutting down this month
item.description: Another high-profile live-service game is shutting down soon after launch: this time it's the free-to-play squad shooter Highguard. Developer Wildlight Entertainment says that, even though...

item.title: Shark’s latest robot vacuum hunts stains with UV light
item.description: The Shark PowerDetect UV Reveal is SharkNinja's latest robot vacuum and mop. A flagship model with a multifunctional dock that empties the dustbin and refills and washes its mop, the Reveal's...

item.title: Claude Code rolls out a voice mode capability
item.description: Anthropic is stepping up its game in the AI coding space with the rollout of Voice Mode in Claude Code.

item.title: Android’s Find Hub adds iPhone-like luggage tracking links
item.description: Google is rolling out an Android update that includes the ability to share the location of your missing luggage with an airline. It's similar to the luggage-tracking feature Apple brought to the...

item.title: Google’s latest Pixel drop allows Gemini to order groceries for you and more
item.description: Google is adding several new features to Pixel phones with its latest March update, including the ability for its Gemini AI assistant to do things for you, like order groceries or book a ride....

item.title: X says it will suspend creators from revenue-sharing program for unlabeled AI posts of ‘armed conflict’
item.description: Creators who break the rules will get a three-month suspension, and if they continue to violate the policy, they'll be permanently banned.

item.title: Fake Tech Support Spam Deploys Customized Havoc C2 Across Organizations
item.description: Threat hunters have called attention to a new campaign as part of which bad actors masqueraded as fake IT support to deliver the Havoc command-and-control (C2) framework as a precursor to data...

item.title: Create new worlds in Project Genie with these 4 tips
item.description: A screen capture of Project Genie, an experimental interface showing a grid of circular images, many of which appear to be 360-degree views, with a large, central black globe labeled Create your own
